examples.description: central chest pain — full ED note: chief complaint, structured HPI, allergies and medications, vital signs each with units, focused exam, investigations (ECG, troponin, chest X-ray), an assessment synthesized and cited from multiple captured findings, and a mandatory admit disposition with follow-up (Grounded Synthesis Mode, v3.2)

transcript.text: What brought you to the emergency department today?
I've had crushing chest pain in the center of my chest for about two hours now.
It started while I was resting, it radiates down my left arm, and it's about an eight out of ten. Nothing I do makes it better.
I feel sweaty and a bit short of breath and nauseated with it. No fever and no cough.
I have high blood pressure and type two diabetes, and I had a stent put in three years ago.
I take aspirin 81 milligrams daily, metoprolol 50 milligrams daily, and metformin 1000 milligrams twice a day. My last dose was this morning.
Any allergies? Yes, penicillin gives me a rash. Nothing else.
Looking at the monitor, blood pressure is 158 over 94 millimeters of mercury, heart rate is 102 beats per minute.
Respiratory rate is 22 breaths per minute, oxygen saturation is 94 percent on room air, and temperature is 37.1 degrees Celsius.
On exam he's diaphoretic and uncomfortable. Heart sounds are regular with no murmur.
On auscultation the chest is clear with good air entry bilaterally and no crackles. The abdomen is soft and non-tender.
The ECG shows ST elevation of two millimeters in leads two, three, and AVF.
The first troponin is back at 0.85 nanograms per milliliter, which is elevated.
The chest X-ray shows no consolidation and no pneumothorax, with a normal cardiac silhouette.
I'm activating the cath lab and admitting him to cardiology. I've given aspirin 325 milligrams chewed and started a heparin infusion at 0210.
Cardiology will follow in the cath lab; family has been updated and he'll go directly for angiography.

claims.text: Pain began at rest, radiates down the left arm, rated 8 out of 10, with nothing relieving it.

claims.source_quote: I feel sweaty and a bit short of breath and nauseated with it. No fever and no cough.

claims.source_quote: I have high blood pressure and type two diabetes, and I had a stent put in three years ago.

claims.source_quote: I take aspirin 81 milligrams daily, metoprolol 50 milligrams daily, and metformin 1000 milligrams twice a day. My last dose was this morning.
Yes, penicillin gives me a rash. Nothing else.

claims.source_quote: Respiratory rate is 22 breaths per minute, oxygen saturation is 94 percent on room air, and temperature is 37.1 degrees Celsius.

claims.text: General: diaphoretic and uncomfortable. Cardiovascular: regular heart sounds with no murmur.
claims.source_quote: On exam he's diaphoretic and uncomfortable. Heart sounds are regular with no murmur.

claims.text: Respiratory: chest clear on auscultation with good air entry bilaterally and no crackles. Abdomen: soft and non-tender.
claims.source_quote: On auscultation the chest is clear with good air entry bilaterally and no crackles. The abdomen is soft and non-tender.

claims.source_quote: The ECG shows ST elevation of two millimeters in leads two, three, and AVF.
The first troponin is back at 0.85 nanograms per milliliter, which is elevated.

claims.text: Chest X-ray: no consolidation, no pneumothorax, normal cardiac silhouette.
claims.source_quote: The chest X-ray shows no consolidation and no pneumothorax, with a normal cardiac silhouette.

claims.text: Working assessment: acute inferior ST-elevation myocardial infarction — ST elevation in the inferior leads on ECG with an elevated troponin, in the setting of central chest pain at rest radiating to the left arm.
claims.source_quote: The ECG shows ST elevation of two millimeters in leads two, three, and AVF.
